title: T-080 — FactorySpec+BOM bundle approval API/actions
prompt:
# T-080 — FactorySpec+BOM bundle approval API/actions

Build the Technical-owned server/API approval path that releases a `factory_specs` record and its shared BOM version as one factory-use bundle. This is the backend/action task only; UI is split to T-090. Implement submit-for-review, get blockers, approve, reject, and explicit split-request handling if policy allows. Approval must verify shared BOM compatibility, RM usability, supplier_specs state, factory_spec version/status through T-081 adapter, Technical approver permission, audit/e-sign evidence when platform support exists, and D365 optional disabled/local mode. RED first: bundle approval cannot partially release unless Technical approver explicitly creates a separate split request; D365 export/preload must not unlock factory use.

pipeline_inputs.description: Backend/action contract for atomic factory_specs + shared BOM approval bundle.
pipeline_inputs.details: One task type: API/actions/service tests. UI panel/modal implementation is T-090. Approval starts from T-079 factory_specs and T-081 release adapter; local dependencies remain 03-TECHNICAL task IDs only. Settings T-122/T-123 authorization dependencies are declared under cross_module_dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Given factory_spec is in_review and BOM is draft/reviewable with passing RM usability, when Technical approver approves bundle, then both move to canonical factory-usable status through T-081 and one audit/evidence bundle id links them.
Given BOM passes but factory_spec fails release blocker, then approval is rejected atomically and neither record is released.
Given D365 integration is disabled, local Technical release still works and D365 sync/export state remains optional integration metadata.
Given released bundle is edited, API creates next draft versions through clone-on-write and keeps released versions immutable.
Given user lacks Technical approval permission from Settings policy, approve/reject actions fail with explicit authorization error.
test_strategy: RED unit/action tests for atomic approve/reject and split prohibition.
RED tests for Settings authorization preflight and D365-disabled local release.
risk_red_lines: Do not create a second BOM model or NPD-owned factory_spec path.
Do not release only one side of the bundle by accident.
Do not require D365 to approve local Technical release.
Do not mutate released versions in place.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Authorization policy schema/seeds for Technical approver permissions.
Authorization helper/action/preflight integration consumed by release actions.
